response.justification: Szabo is exceptionally well-aligned with the role's core context—he's currently a Staff engineer working directly in AI-agentic infrastructure with MCP frameworks and code generators, which is the novel baseline of this position. He has demonstrated distributed systems expertise from handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby and scaling architecture work across multiple platforms. However, Python is listed as a must-have and is entirely absent from his resume; while his history of rapid language adoption (DLang, Java, C++, TypeScript, Swift) suggests he could acquire it, this gap warrants clarification in interview before proceeding.
strengths: Currently working in AI-native environment with agentic code generation and MCP frameworks—directly aligned with the role's core operating model
Demonstrated distributed systems and high-throughput architecture expertise from Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec) and Optilyz (millions of mails annually)
Staff-level owner with proven operational mindset, infrastructure experience, and ability to work across full stack from product to DevOps
concerns: Python is listed as a must-have skill but is completely absent from the resume; TypeScript is strong but Python proficiency is undemonstrated
Broad technical range across many languages and roles (DLang, Java, C++, JavaScript, Swift) may indicate less deep expertise in any single domain compared to specialists
Career shows several short tenures (e.g., 6 months at WatchUp, 1.5 years at National Instruments); pattern of frequent role transitions could indicate instability

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and high-throughput architecture depth
